question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: setIsRootRatified / event caller field] Can an unprivileged operator under the precondition that operator calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) on behalf of maker trigger the event SetIsRootRatified(operator, maker, root, true) where caller != maker, violating the invariant that off-chain indexers correctly attribute the ratification to the maker (not the caller), causing scoped impact: no test verifies the event fields when caller != maker and the comment does not document that the caller field in the event may differ from the maker field, creating a documentation gap for off-chain consumers? Proof idea: unit test setIsRootRatified called by an authorized operator and assert the emitted event has caller == operator and maker == maker.
